summary: Excellent audience recording of a hot ’84 Berkeley run, highlighted by smoking Cumberland and Meet Me At The Bottom.
review: The show is widely praised for its adventurous setlist, tight improvisation and standout moments like a blistering “Cumberland” and a smoky “Meet Me At The Bottom.” Reviewers note the audience‑tape captures the energy well despite the venue’s acoustics, delivering clear and lively sound. Even with a few missteps (the “Why Don’t We Do It In The Road” cover), the performance is considered one of the better shows of that six‑night stretch.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers gave 4–5 stars and highlighted strong musical moments; recording quality is repeatedly described as excellent audience tape, yielding a combined high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the audience recording "amazing" and note that the authorized taping spot avoided the venue’s problematic acoustics; overall clear, lively mix with no major drop‑outs.

poor_songs: Why Don't We Do It In The Road
show_quality.setlist_flow: The six‑night run at Berkeley Community Theater avoided repeats, delivering fresh surprises each night; this final show kept the momentum with strong improvisation and a tight sequence of covers and originals.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for shredding "Cumberland," flying guitar work, and a memorable descending progression in the lights.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as a monster on the low end, offering solid bass foundation and attempting to ease into "Lovelight."
band_member_comments.Bob: Delivered a new blues cover and a hot rendition of "Gloria."
band_member_comments.Drums: Mentioned only in passing (after drums came "Uncle John's"), no specific praise or criticism.
